  "summary": "This chunk contains several necromantic experiments from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), including rituals for revealing thieves via spirit conjuration, discovering hidden treasure in dreams, invoking spirits like Semiforas and Haram, obtaining a horse through binding oaths, achieving invisibility using dove blood and lamb-skin paper, and chemical prescriptions. It includes detailed protective circles with Trinitarian inscriptions, lists of spirit names (e.g., Astaroth, Asef Caroth, Layalym), and incantation formulas invoking divine authority. The text reflects strong Christian framing (crucifixion wounds, Trinity invocations) alongside magical formulaic repetition.",
  "key_passages": [
    "Coniuro vos prenominatos demones • per magistrum vestrum Astaroth, vt compareatis in forma furis et furti...",
    "hoc est nomen magnum Semiforas, quod erat scriptum in fronte Aaron, sacerdotis creatoris.",
    "per Sotuem, Sortfen, vt in nocte sequenti veniat ad me in sompnis Haram, spiritus benignus...",
    "Nunc igitur sume albam columbam, die Sabbati de nocte, et cartam agni virgineam... scribe de huiuscuscumque sangwine hanc figuram.",
    "Protege, salua, benedic, sanctifica populum cunctum per crucis signum domini. Morbos auerte corporis et anime."
  ]
}
